prompt: The log shipper is shipping garbage and I'm losing my mind. Receiver-side we see two flavors of nonsense: most batches bounce with `wire: corrupt frame: declared payload 34 bytes, frame carries 63` style errors, and the batches that DO land decode as the same record repeated — always whatever was logged LAST in that interval, so the invoice audit log for Tuesday is 4000 copies of 'retrying webhook'. It gets better: when an upload fails and the shipper retries the batch after new traffic has come in, the retried batch arrives containing the NEW records, not the ones that failed. Encode-one-decode-one in isolation is spotless, CRCs and all, which is why this sat unnoticed until finance diffed the audit log. Everything lives in wire.go (encoder, batch, decoder). wire_test.go now reproduces all of it — leave the tests alone, find what's actually wrong and fix it, then show me `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` green.
